title: PHP match Expression (PHP 8)
content:
### PHP match Expression

Introduced in PHP 8, `match` is an expression-based alternative to `switch` that returns a value.

### Syntax

```php
<?php
$result = match (expression) {
    value1 => return1,
    value2 => return2,
    default => defaultValue,
};
?>
```

### Example

```php
<?php
$statusCode = 404;

$message = match ($statusCode) {
    200 => "OK",
    301, 302 => "Redirect",
    404 => "Not Found",
    500 => "Server Error",
    default => "Unknown status",
};

echo $message; // Not Found
?>
```

### match vs switch

| Feature | match | switch |
|---------|-------|--------|
| Returns value | Yes | No |
| Strict comparison | Yes (`===`) | No (`==`) |
| Break required | No | Yes |
| Multiple values | Comma-separated | Fall-through |
| Exhaustive | Throws on unmatched | Falls to default |

> **Key Point:** `match` uses strict comparison (`===`), returns a value, and throws an `UnhandledMatchError` if no arm matches and no default is provided.

quiz.question: Does match require a break statement?
What type of comparison does match use?
